Step for Winsorization

Source: R/map_recipe_timewise.R
step_winsorize.Rd

step_winsorize() creates a recipe step that applies winsorization to numeric variables. Winsorization replaces extreme values below or above specified quantile thresholds with those threshold values.

This step is useful to reduce the influence of outliers without fully trimming observations.

Usage

step_winsorize(
  recipe,
  ...,
  role = NA,
  trained = FALSE,
  probs = c(0.05, 0.95),
  skip = FALSE,
  id = recipes::rand_id("winsorize")
)

Arguments

recipe

A recipe object. The step will be added to the sequence of operations for this recipe.

...

One or more selector functions to choose which variables to apply winsorization to. If empty, all numeric variables are selected.

role

Not used by this step since no new variables are created. Defaults to NA.

trained

A logical indicating whether the step has been trained (i.e., thresholds have been calculated).

probs

A numeric vector of length 2 indicating the lower and upper quantile probabilities used to determine winsorization limits. Default is c(0.05, 0.95).

skip

A logical. Should the step be skipped when the recipe is baked by recipes::bake()? Default is FALSE.

id

A character string used to identify this step. Defaults to a random string generated by recipes::rand_id("winsorize").

Value

An updated recipe object with the step_winsorize added to the sequence of steps.

Details

The actual winsorization limits are computed during the prep() phase using the quantiles of the training data. During the bake() phase, all values below the lower quantile threshold are replaced with that threshold, and values above the upper quantile threshold are similarly adjusted.

See also

map_recipe_timewise for applying a recipe (including this step) one date at a time; recipes::recipe, recipes::prep and recipes::bake for the underlying framework.
